title: Walking from Deary on railroad tracks; school in Helmer; worked for board in Deary
description: Walking home from Deary on the railroad track in snow. Went to school in Helmer until the snow flew. Worked for board in Deary and went to school, but it was hard work. Enjoyed Mrs. Wesby's treated like one of the family.

title: Tomboy rides horses standing up; didn't go to dances; traded for groceries in Bovill; hauled hay on sled in the winter
description: A tomboy—would ride horses standing up. Didn't go to dances often because didn't have the clothes or money. Traded eggs for groceries at Bovill. Could only plant things in garden that could take a frost. In fall picked up other garden goods from ridges. One summer they got such a full wagon load of produce from near Kendrick that they had to block the wagon on the way home so the horses could rest. Hauled hay on sled one winter during WW I to feed stock.

title: Worked for logging camp at Shea Meadows; boss fired for drinking
description: Worked as flunkey for logging camp at Shea Meadows. Boss got mad at her and May for going to a show with the boys. A week later the boss was fired for drinking on the job.

title: Hired at Camp 1 near Princeton; cook fired for giving food away
description: Nogle came out next year and hired them as flunkeys at Camp 1 near Princeton. Worked for German cook who was fired for giving food away to friends. Next cook wanted male flunkies.

title: Went to Shea Mountain; no talking in dining rooms; went to dance at Kennedy Ford; scrubbing floors
description: Went to Shea Meadows and Al "Shorty" Justice and Charlie Justice were the cooks. German cook: flies, no talking in dining rooms, got mad when they went to dance at Kennedy Ford. Husband's cooking was more like home cooking. Cut his own meat. Quite a few cooks didn't want women flunkeys. Albert preferred women, he thought they kept things up better. Scrubbing floors once a week with a broom.

title: Old logging camps; separate tables for Italians; snipe hunting; boss pushed her off a bridge
description: Old logging camps. Separate tables for Italians. Going snipe hunting. Two flunkeys scuffle with Albert at Elk River. Boss pushed her off bridge and she grabbed his legs and dragged him in also. May and Molley throw the clerk in the water.

title: Lumberjacks respected women and stood up for them
description: Lumberjacks respected women and would stand up for them. Albert and Charlie fixed the scale so it looked like they were losing weight.

title: Breakfast and lunch; starved by night; stove had three ovens; baking in the morning
description: Breakfast at 6 and some ate in five minutes. Jacks fixed their own lunch from a spread on one table. Starved by night—girls kept busy filling plates. Stove had three ovens. Tried to get baking done in the morning.
